mapping.a_element: transition operators (T operators) describing scattering properties of objects
mapping.b_element: multiple scattering formulation describing interactions between bodies

mapping.a_element: Casimir energy expressed in terms of scattering data
mapping.b_element: Casimir energies derived from scattering interactions between bodies

mapping.a_element: scatterers in 1d and 3d, including spherical bodies
mapping.b_element: semitransparent spheres and cylinders as scattering bodies

mapping.a_element: utilization of scattering data to compute Casimir force
mapping.b_element: explicit calculation of Casimir energies using scattering expressions

mapping.a_element: convergence properties of the scattering-based formula
mapping.b_element: power series expansions and exact summation in weak coupling limit

brief_justification: Both papers employ a scattering-based framework to compute Casimir forces, where the core machinery is the use of scattering operators (T operators or multiple scattering formulations) to express Casimir energy in terms of interactions between objects. The role of scattering data and its manipulation to derive forces or energies is fundamentally the same in both approaches.
